title:
Senate - Rules (Room 321)
Adjourned
2026-02-18 16:55-16:59
full_text: All right. Good afternoon, evening. Everyone. We're just going to have a really brief meeting at our last meeting. We heard H.J.M. 3 as a subcommittee. I'll go to Senator Stewart. Madam Chair, I move that we do a do pass on House Joint Memorial 3. All right. Is there any objection to that motion? The man show all members present voting in the affirmative and that's a do pass. And we have Senator Thompson here on HB 287. Go ahead, Senator. Thank you. Representative, I gave you a promotion. So no comment at this moment. I'll wait after the vote. So Madam Chair, thank you for hearing this. This bill is to create a permanent staffed legislative health and human services committee. I think probably most people on this committee understand the need for it. I had a legra Hernandez from LFC pulver numbers for the money that we spent on health and human services in FY 2025. It was 14.4 billion dollars. And we're trying to look at the things involved with that in our spare type. And that's all I have, Madam Chair. Thank you, Senator Hermione. Motion for a do pass. Senator Thomas. Did anybody put in the house bill to Madam Chair for this? Actually, Madam Chair, there is money in house bill one for interim expenses. And we feel like that's enough. We the date of the implementation of this is now January 1st. So what we plan to do in the interim is to work on a job description for a director and then slowly build that up. There is money in interim expenses. And so Madam Chair, Representative Thompson, what about space? Mr. Matt, I'm sorry. Madam Chair, that's going to be an issue. But we personally don't feel like space should be a reason for us not to be able to do our jobs the best way we can. I agree. We'll make it work somehow. I agree. Thank you. I mean, we'll support. Thank you. Senator Stewart. And I'm in support also, but that was going to be my question. Space is a huge issue. Now that we have these district legislative eights. We need space for HR and other people and let's counsel. And we're not being very nice to let counsel. We have not given them good space and it's one of the reasons why they can't get people to come and work for them. Because there is no space for them. So I just want everybody to think about that because it's got to have some given take before we get that executive office building where we can move people out of our legislative space. So we can do that. That's going to be several years. So you can have to get creative with that. And I've got some ideas. Thank you. I'll talk to you about it. Any further debate before we go to that motion? Seeing none. Is there any opposition to the motion? Seeing none, you have do pass. Thank you so much for meeting. Passing in. Absolutely. And members before you leave, two things. So one, today is Rebecca, our Zoom operator's perfect. Thank you. And two, I know many of you know her plans. Her has been working for this committee and serving us amazingly for 20 years this year. So we have dessert. Oh, you're the nicer ones. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. At 16 years in judiciary and 20 years in rules. So thank you, Herbert. I'd like to say also as a former chair of this committee. I also want to say thank you, Herbert, for what you, the patience you've had. Enduring with us over the years too. So thank you also. Yes. All right. Thank you all. That's our business. And rules is adjourned for until next year. Thank you, Herbert.
